docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:15:and wire path form (`path_posix.rs`/`path_safety.rs`). Everything else is policy
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:159:  - divergence: Field-by-field the three copies agree today: 30s inner connect_timeout, 30s outer tokio::time::timeout, and nothing else (no keepalive, no nodelay, no windows, no decode limit). Divergence is structural: blit-app names the constant (CONNECT_TIMEOUT, client.rs:24) and includes the duration in its timeout error; the two blit-core copies hardcode Duration::from_secs(30) twice each and omit the duration from the error text. The identical 'audit-2' comment was pasted into all three, proving a fix already had to be applied three times — the next config change (e.g. client keepalive) is one missed copy away from divergence. blit-core does not use blit-app's helper (dependency direction forbids it), so ownership cannot consolidate where the copies live today.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:164:  - sites: crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:71 (DATA_PLANE_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT/DATA_PLANE_TOKEN_TIMEOUT); crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:698 (PULL_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT/PULL_TOKEN_TIMEOUT); crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:597 (PULL_SYNC_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT/PULL_SYNC_TOKEN_TIMEOUT); crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:719 (ACCEPT_TIMEOUT/TOKEN_TIMEOUT — second copy in the same file)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:196:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:239 — gRPC channel builder #1 (RemotePullClient::connect) — Endpoint::from_shared + .connect_timeout(30s) + outer tokio::time::timeout(30s). No keepalive, no nodelay, no window/decode-size config. Independently written copy with the same 'audit-2' comment block as the push copy.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:205:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:597 — TCP data-plane server accept (pull-sync streaming path) — Third local copy of the same pair: PULL_SYNC_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT=30s / PULL_SYNC_TOKEN_TIMEOUT=15s (597-598); no socket options set; socket handed to DataPlaneSession::from_stream at 641.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:206:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:719 — TCP data-plane server accept (pull-sync resume path) — fourth copy of the timeout pair — ACCEPT_TIMEOUT=30s / TOKEN_TIMEOUT=15s defined AGAIN at 719-720 in the same file as 597-598; no socket options; DataPlaneSession::from_stream at 765.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:223:No single module owns timeouts & liveness. The closest thing to an owner is crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/stall_guard.rs (StallGuard/StallGuardWriter + the owner-decided 30s TRANSFER_STALL_TIMEOUT), but it deliberately covers only raw-TCP data-plane progress, and at least four other strata each grew their own local policy: blit-daemon's net_timeout::within helper, blit-app's client.rs CONNECT_TIMEOUT, the TUI's SUBSCRIBE_OPEN/SNAPSHOT_FETCH constants, and the prometheus bridge's 5/8/10s set — while the two blit-core channel builders carry bare inline 30s literals. The smear is asymmetric in a dangerous way: connects and daemon-side accepts are well bounded (30s/15s, declared four separate times), but once connected, every client-side gRPC await in the workspace is structurally unbounded — no client channel sets HTTP/2 keepalive, TCP keepalive, or a per-RPC deadline, so delegation progress (remote.rs:734), push responses (helpers.rs:245), all three pull fallback loops (pull.rs:330/505/790), and `blit jobs watch` by default will wait forever on a silently-dead daemon; only the daemon server protects itself (main.rs:138). The sharpest single problem is that the planned fix — the audit-h3c slice-2 progress watchdog — exists only as comments plus a pass-through helper (grpc_fallback.rs:150), and its own TODOs record that the surrounding error-conversion sites already strip the error chain the watchdog would need for retries to work. Secondary findings: raw TCP data-plane connects are unbounded (pull.rs:1710, data_plane.rs:92) while control-plane connects are triple-bounded; pull_sync.rs redefines its own accept/token constants twice in one file under a comment claiming reuse; and the two set_keepalive(true) calls run at OS-default ~2h timing while comments claim they prevent idle-stream timeouts.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:227:- HEADLINE — no client channel in the workspace configures HTTP/2 keepalive, TCP keepalive, or a per-RPC deadline (verified: the only keepalive is the daemon server, main.rs:138-139; no Endpoint anywhere calls .timeout()/.tcp_keepalive()/.http2_keep_alive_interval()). Consequence: every long-lived client-side gRPC await — delegation progress (blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:734, :878), push response forwarder (blit-core/src/remote/push/client/helpers.rs:245 feeding mod.rs:439), all three pull fallback receive loops (blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:330, :505, :790), RemoteFileStream (pull.rs:1232), `blit jobs watch` at default --timeout-secs=0 (blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:305), and the TUI forwarder (blit-tui/src/main.rs:5717) — hangs forever against a silently-dead daemon (kill -9 survives via FIN, but lid-close/partition/NAT-drop does not). Violates RELIABLE ('fails plainly').
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:232:- set_keepalive(true) at default OS timing (~2h idle on Linux) is functionally inert yet documented in both copies as preventing idle-stream timeouts (data_plane.rs:101-108, daemon push/data_plane.rs:112-120); the daemon copy silently swallows failure. Misleading comments are a RELIABLE risk: future authors will assume liveness coverage that does not exist.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:239:  - divergence: Values agree (30s/30s everywhere) but form diverges: blit-app names CONNECT_TIMEOUT as a pub const with a distinct error string; both blit-core copies use bare inline literals and identical near-duplicate comment blocks; the daemon adds a redundant third 30s wrap (within(SOURCE_CONNECT_TIMEOUT, ...)) around an already double-bounded connect. None of the three builders sets HTTP/2 keepalive, TCP keepalive, per-RPC Endpoint::timeout, or decode limits — so the agreement is also an agreement to leave every post-connect await unbounded.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:251:  - divergence: Same call, divergent error handling: core copy logs failure via log::warn (POST_REVIEW_FIXES §1.1); daemon copy discards it with `let _ =`. Both pass no keepalive parameters, so OS defaults apply (~2h idle on Linux) — the comments claim it 'prevents idle stream timeouts during long transfers' but at default timing it is effectively inert as a liveness mechanism.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:271:- `JobsWatchArgs::timeout_secs default` = 0 = wait forever (user flag --timeout-secs) (crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:142) — SIMPLE: an explicit user-tuned timeout knob on a tool whose principle is zero tuning; RELIABLE: the default (0) plus no client keepalive means `blit jobs watch` hangs forever on a vanished daemon.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:272:- `set_keepalive(true) with OS-default timing` = true (no interval params; Linux default ≈ 2h idle); second copy daemon push/data_plane.rs:120 with silent `let _ =` (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/data_plane.rs:106) — RELIABLE: comments oversell it ('prevents idle stream timeouts'); at default kernel timing it detects nothing inside any realistic transfer window.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:279:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:239 — gRPC channel builder copy #1 (RemotePullClient::connect): connect_timeout(30s) inline literal + outer tokio::time::timeout(30s) at :242. No HTTP/2 keepalive, no TCP keepalive, no per-RPC timeout.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:286:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs:307 — gRPC channel builder copy #2 (RemotePushClient::connect): identical inline 30s connect_timeout + outer 30s timeout (:310). No keepalive, no per-RPC timeout.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:305:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/client.rs:24 — gRPC channel builder copy #3: named CONNECT_TIMEOUT=30s const, connect_timeout + outer timeout (:40-44) (audit-2/2a). Only copy with a named constant and a distinct timeout error message. Still no keepalive / per-RPC timeout.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:330:- Network BufferPool memory is unbounded by system memory: pool formula at push/client/mod.rs:125-128 / blit-daemon/service/pull.rs:685-688 authorizes up to ~8.5 GiB budget (32 streams x 64 MiB x (2*streams+4) x 2) with no sysinfo check, while the local-copy BufferSizer carefully caps at 10% of available RAM (buffer.rs:84). The two sizing regimes embody opposite philosophies and the riskier one governs the bigger allocations.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:346:- [DIVERGE] double-buffered overlap copy loop (read one buffer while writing the other)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:351:  - divergence: Value agrees (256 KiB) in all five copies, but the daemon copy is a stack array ([0u8; 256*1024]) while checksum.rs uses heap vecs; the daemon also reimplements Blake3 file hashing instead of calling checksum::hash_file.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:375:- `checksum hash buffer` = 256 KiB (x4 here + stack copy at blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:524) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/checksum.rs:148) — SIMPLE: five copy-pasted literals; daemon's stack variant adds a 256 KiB frame on an async-adjacent path
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:378:- `chunked_copy large-file override` = >1 GiB -> 16 MiB (bypasses BufferSizer) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/chunked.rs:35) — SIMPLE: hardcoded special case that overrides the supposed sizing authority for exactly the files where sizing matters most
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:399:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:506 — consumer (local copy inside remote sink) — write_file_payload local-copy path constructs BufferSizer::default() per file — local sizing regime used inside the remote pipeline.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:409:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/resume.rs:16 — constant definition — DEFAULT_BLOCK_SIZE = 1 MiB, MAX_BLOCK_SIZE = 64 MiB (19); two vec![0u8; block_size] buffers per resume copy (82-83).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:414:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/zero_copy.rs:69 — constant (kernel path) — MAX_SPLICE = 8 MiB per splice call — separate sizing regime for the zero-copy path.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:418:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:524 — read buffer — build_file_header checksum loop uses a 256 KiB STACK array ([0u8; 256*1024]) — same value as checksum.rs's heap vecs, different mechanism, fifth copy of the 256 KiB decision.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:420:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:685 — pool construction formula (daemon pull) — accept_and_wrap_sinks: buffer_size = chunk_bytes.max(64KiB); pool_size = streams*2+4; budget = buffer_size*pool_size*2 — verbatim copy of push/client/mod.rs:125-128.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:423:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:789 — read buffer (resume blocks) — vec![0u8; block_size] for block reads; block_size = client param or DEFAULT_BLOCK_SIZE (680-684); second copy at 987 with same 919-923 defaulting.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:440:- Same failure, different retryability depending on route: a connection-refused daemon is classifier-visible (retryable) through blit-app/client.rs:44 (.with_context preserves the chain) but classifier-blind (fatal) through pull.rs:245 and push/client/mod.rs:313 (string-formatted). So `blit copy --retry 3` retries a refused delegated/remote-remote connect but not a refused direct push/pull connect. Configuration divergence between three independently-written builders, exactly the seed pattern.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:452:  - divergence: Direct contradictions kind-by-kind: ConnectionRefused, UnexpectedEof, NotConnected are Retryable in the live blit-app list (retry.rs:40-44) but explicitly Fatal in blit-core (errors.rs:108-113, 'could go either way - default to fatal'). Interrupted and WouldBlock are Retryable in blit-core (errors.rs:94-98) but absent (= fatal) in blit-app. Only TimedOut/ConnectionReset/ConnectionAborted/BrokenPipe agree. The dead copy also carries an unused attempts/should_retry budget model (errors.rs:66-74) that the live loop reimplements differently (attempt_no counter in run_with_retries).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:470:- `CONNECT_TIMEOUT` = Duration::from_secs(30) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/client.rs:24) — The only named/shared copy of the connect deadline; the core-crate builders don't use it (dependency direction prevents it — blit-core can't import blit-app), evidence ownership sits in the wrong crate.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:496:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/client.rs:37 — CLASSIFIER-VISIBLE connect (divergent twin): connect_with_timeout uses .with_context (line 44), PRESERVING the tonic transport error chain (which contains the io::Error); timeout Elapsed still a plain string (43); CONNECT_TIMEOUT=30s at 24 — Channel builder duplicate #3. Only this copy lets is_retryable see ConnectionRefused at connect time. Used by delegated remote-to-remote (remote.rs:839) and admin verbs.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:500:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/control.rs:328 — Plain-fail messaging: failed_precondition on incomplete mirror scan tells the user to 'Resolve the unreadable source path(s) and retry' — Human-directed retry advice; correctly NOT machine-retryable.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:508:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-tui/src/exec_plan.rs:49 — TUI site (recorded only): comment asserting interrupted local copy 'only under-copies, which is safe and retryable' — No actual retry mechanism behind it.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:537:  - divergence: All three bound connect at 30s, but the two core copies stringify the transport error (eyre!("failed to connect to {}: {}")) destroying the source chain — a ConnectionRefused there is invisible to is_retryable — and their timeout text omits the duration. The app copy uses with_context (chain preserved), includes the duration, and names the constant; core never adopted it.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:550:- `CONNECT_TIMEOUT` = Duration::from_secs(30) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/client.rs:24) — SIMPLE: fixed, no user tuning needed — fine. RELIABLE tension: it is the named, documented copy of a value that also exists as four anonymous literals in blit-core; a future change here will not propagate.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:551:- `(unnamed connect timeout, pull)` = std::time::Duration::from_secs(30) (also :242) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:241) — RELIABLE: duplicate of CONNECT_TIMEOUT as a bare literal; its timeout error text ('connecting to {} timed out', :244) omits the duration, so the user is not told how long blit waited.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:565:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/client.rs:37 — Channel-builder #3 (connect_with_timeout): the GOOD copy — with_context preserves the error chain (:39, :44), timeout message includes the duration (:43), named CONNECT_TIMEOUT const. Only the app/CLI control-plane uses it; core's two builders never adopted it.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:580:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/src/completions.rs:84 — CLI-layer copy of the status.message() stringify pattern.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:584:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/helpers.rs:183 — record_unreadable_entry: per-file failure text "[push] skipping '{}' ({})" printed red to stderr AND accumulated into the unreadable list that gates mirror deletion (R46-F2).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:600:- Daemon TransferProgress/GetState byte counters are only wired for DelegatedPull (core.rs:706); push/pull/pull_sync handlers never call job.bytes_counter() (push receive passes None at service/push/data_plane.rs:842), so the 10 Hz ticker broadcasts bytes_completed=0/throughput_bps=0 forever for those kinds, and 'blit jobs watch' renders 'bytes=0 throughput=0 B' for most real transfers — indistinguishable from a stalled transfer.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:604:- Local transfers (the flagship local->local path) have no real progress pipeline at all: the CLI shows an indeterminate spinner (local.rs:98-115); the plumbing that pretends otherwise is dead — LocalMirrorOptions.progress is written (local.rs:183) but never read (options.rs:48 is the only core reference), and chunked_copy_file's indicatif::ProgressBar parameter has zero callers and would only set_position after the copy finishes (chunked.rs:18,48-50). Bonus layering smell: blit-core depends on indicatif (blit-core/Cargo.toml:21) solely for this dead parameter.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:617:  - divergence: gRPC inline path: Payload per chunk + FileComplete(path, 0) ('bytes already counted'). Unified TCP receive pipeline: Payload(0, whole-file bytes_written) + FileComplete(path, bytes_written) — FileComplete carries 0 on one path and the full byte count on the other, and granularity is per-chunk vs per-file. Tar-shard arm (:247-249) emits Payload only, so files in shards are never file-counted on receive.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:620:  - divergence: Same saturating_sub pattern written twice; blit-app guards monotonicity with max(), daemon with atomic swap. Both correct in isolation — but the blit-app copy is dead (no BytesProgress on the wire).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:642:- `TUI throughput warm-up` = 1.0 s suppression (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-tui/src/progress_accum.rs:99) — none (defensible smoothing) — but exists only in the TUI copy of the throughput math, not the CLI or daemon copies.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:658:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:233 — Producer (TCP receive, FILE record): emits BOTH report_payload(0, bytes_written) AND report_file_complete(path, bytes_written) for the same file — Double-emit; tar-shard arm at :247-249 emits Payload only.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:682:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/chunked.rs:18 — DEAD + layering violation: chunked_copy_file takes Option<&indicatif::ProgressBar> (zero callers in workspace) and only calls pb.set_position AFTER the whole io::copy (:48-50); blit-core Cargo.toml:21 depends on indicatif (a terminal UI crate) for this
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:758:- `chunked_copy 1 GiB→16 MiB branch` = file_size > 1_073_741_824 → 16 MiB fixed (crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/chunked.rs:35) — SIMPLE: bypasses BufferSizer (including its memory cap) for the biggest files with a frozen value that happens to equal BufferSizer's max — redundant divergent decision.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:759:- `Windows no-buffering thresholds` = threshold 1 GiB, floor 2 GiB, headroom 512 MiB, small-file max 512 MiB (crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:20) — SIMPLE: platform cache-bypass policy frozen at authoring; partially memory-aware via floor/headroom.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:760:- `TINY/HUGE fast-path thresholds` = 256 files / 256 MiB total / 1 GiB single (crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/fast_path.rs:11) — SIMPLE: routing tuned by authoring intuition; sits beside a perf-history system that could inform it but doesn't.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:770:- `retry/wait CLI defaults` = retry=0, wait=5s, fixed spacing (run_with_retries, blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:55) (crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:278) — SIMPLE: an explicit user knob in a zero-tuning tool (owner-approved robocopy parity); no backoff — fixed 5s spacing is a frozen recovery policy.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:786:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:685 — Deprecated multi-stream Pull RPC pool sizing: same streams*2+4 formula as push client; PULL_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT=30s / PULL_TOKEN_TIMEOUT=15s (698-699) — Third copy of the pool-sizing formula.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:789:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/client.rs:37 — gRPC channel builder #3 (app/admin): connect_with_timeout using named CONNECT_TIMEOUT=30s (line 24) inner+outer; no keepalive, no decode-limit override — Only this copy has a named constant; ~15 call sites across blit-app admin verbs, transfers/remote.rs:694,839, blit-cli/completions.rs:74.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:798:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/buffer.rs:24 — BufferSizer: min 1 MiB / max 16 MiB, network base 8 MiB vs local 4 MiB, small<10 MB / medium<=100 MB tiers, linear scale over 900 MiB span, cap 10% of available memory, floor 8 KiB — Half-adaptive: reads real available memory (sysinfo) but the curve breakpoints are frozen. Used by local copy paths (chunked.rs, file_copy/mod.rs:74, parallel.rs, local_worker.rs:29).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:801:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:20 — Windows no-buffering policy: threshold 1 GiB, floor 2 GiB, headroom 512 MiB, small-file cache max 512 MiB — Platform tuning frozen at authoring; memory-aware only via the floor/headroom arithmetic.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:810:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:55 — run_with_retries — retry count/wait come from CLI flags (default retry=0, wait=5s per blit-cli/src/cli.rs:633-639); fixed (non-backoff) spacing — User-tunable knob (mild SIMPLE tension, owner-approved robocopy parity); constant wait rather than exponential backoff.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:821:No single module owns platform behavior. blit-core has three partial owners that do not talk to each other: win_fs.rs (a Windows toolbox where four of eight functions — create_symlink, has_symlink_privilege, compare_paths_case_insensitive, is_reserved_name — are dead, and ensure_long_path is called from exactly one probe site), fs_capability/ (a trait abstraction whose fast_copy half is dead and contains a second, divergent CopyFileExW wrapper), and path_posix.rs/path_safety.rs (the one genuinely healthy owner, for wire path form). Everything else — copy cascade, sinks, deletion loops, change journal, diagnostics — makes its own cfg decisions inline, producing nine duplicate groups of which five disagree (CopyFileExW config, readonly-clearing on four deletion executors, casefold keys, \\?\-prefix stripping, journal canonicalization). The sharpest problem is that Windows parity is asserted (AGENTS.md §5, run-blit-tests.ps1) but structurally unverifiable: every remote integration test is #[cfg(unix)], the ps1 script is manual and not in CI, long-path support exists only as an orphaned helper while every real I/O path passes raw >260-char-fragile paths, and the wire protocol's Unix-only permission model silently drops all Windows metadata in both directions. blit-cli/src, blit-tui/src, and blit-prometheus-bridge contain zero platform-specific code (verified by cfg search), so the smear is confined to blit-core, blit-daemon, and blit-app.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:827:- Mirror deletion behaves differently per transfer direction on Windows: orchestrator.rs:974-997 and daemon admin.rs:197-199 clear the read-only attribute before deleting; delegated_pull.rs:445-498 and blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:203-263 do not. A read-only destination file is deleted by local mirror and push mirror but errors (delete_listed_paths hard-fails the transfer; apply_delete_list's remove_dir failure is silently dropped at line 498) on pull mirror — same user command, three outcomes.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:830:- CasefoldKey's ASCII-only lowercase (mirror_planner.rs:40) vs NTFS's Unicode case-insensitivity means a non-ASCII case-variant pair (e.g. 'Ä.txt' vs 'ä.txt') is treated as two distinct entries — the mirror planner can schedule deletion of a destination file that the filesystem considers identical to a source file it just wrote. macOS case-insensitive volumes get no casefold at all (cfg(not(windows)) exact-path key).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:831:- FilesystemCapability::fast_copy is a dead trait half with a second, strictly worse CopyFileExW implementation (fs_capability/windows.rs:230) and a Unix twin (unix.rs fast_copy); a future caller reaching for the 'capability abstraction' gets no block clone, no buffering heuristic, and no fallback — configuration divergence lying in wait, the exact gRPC-channel-builder pattern repeated for file copy.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:832:- cached_probe (fs_capability/mod.rs:96-117) caches per-device only under cfg(unix); the cfg(not(unix)) branch re-runs probe_capabilities — and volume_info_for_path's GetVolumePathNameW/GetVolumeInformationW — on every call. FAST tension on exactly the platform with the slowest filesystem metadata calls (partially mitigated by the separate BLOCK_CLONE_CACHE in fs_capability/windows.rs:31).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:834:- zero_copy.rs is a fully dead Linux-only module (zero callers; daemon bytes_zero_copy stats are constant 0) — strata debris that misleads readers into thinking splice-based zero-copy is wired into the TCP data plane.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:840:  - divergence: windows_copyfile: ReFS block-clone attempt first, adaptive COPY_FILE_NO_BUFFERING flag (memory-aware heuristic), fs::copy fallback on failure. try_copyfileex: COPYFILE_FLAGS(0), no clone, no NO_BUFFERING, no fallback, errors out. try_copyfileex is reachable only via FilesystemCapability::fast_copy which has zero callers (dead), but it is the obvious trap for the next caller of the trait.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:852:  - divergence: CasefoldKey: POSIX-form join + to_ascii_lowercase (Windows only; exact PathBuf elsewhere). win_fs helper: normpath-normalized native separators + Unicode to_lowercase. Same two paths can compare equal under one and unequal under the other ('Foo\\Bar' vs 'Foo/Bar'). Already documented as h-paths-2 in docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-paths.md.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:855:  - divergence: four independently written deletion loops; only two clear the Windows read-only attribute first. Local mirror and push-side purge delete read-only files; delegated-pull mirror and client-side pull-mirror purge fail with access-denied on the same files. Error surfacing also differs: orchestrator aggregates failures, delegated_pull silently ignores remove_dir errors (line 498 `let _ =`).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:878:- `CasefoldKey fold algorithm` = to_ascii_lowercase() (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/mirror_planner.rs:40) — RELIABLE: ASCII-only fold under-approximates NTFS Unicode case-insensitivity — non-ASCII case pairs look 'extraneous' to mirror deletion on Windows; and macOS case-insensitive APFS gets no fold at all
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:887:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/win_fs.rs:85 — dead code / duplicate — compare_paths_case_insensitive — zero callers; disagrees with mirror_planner CasefoldKey (native separators + Unicode lowercase vs POSIX-form + ASCII lowercase); already flagged in docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-paths.md h-paths-2
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:889:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/win_fs.rs:186 — Windows permission model (deletion) — clear_readonly_recursive — called from orchestrator.rs:975/997, daemon admin.rs:199; NOT called from delegated_pull or blit-app pull-mirror purge
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:890:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:331 — Windows copy engine — windows_copyfile: ReFS block clone (FSCTL_DUPLICATE_EXTENTS_TO_FILE) → CopyFileExW with adaptive NO_BUFFERING → fs::copy fallback. Passes raw paths (no ensure_long_path); deep trees >260 chars fail unless OS-level LongPathsEnabled
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:892:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/mod.rs:41 — per-OS copy cascade decision — cfg(windows): windows_copyfile first (skips preserve_metadata if block clone succeeded), else FILE_FLAG_SEQUENTIAL_SCAN handles + block clone + sparse_copy_windows; cfg(macos): clonefile→fcopyfile→buffered; cfg(unix non-mac): copy_file_range→sendfile→sparse→buffered
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:894:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/clone.rs:190 — Windows sparse copy — mark_file_sparse (FSCTL_SET_SPARSE) + sparse_copy_windows zero-run detection; Unix sparse path (attempt_sparse_copy_unix) uses SEEK_HOLE — different sparse heuristics per OS
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:895:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/mmap.rs:86 — parity fallback — cfg(not(unix)) mmap_copy_file is just std::fs::copy — no Windows fast path despite the name
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:905:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/path_posix.rs:36 — wire path-form owner — relative_path_to_posix: component-walk join with '/' — correct on Windows (splits on \) and POSIX (preserves literal backslash filenames); the documented canonical chokepoint
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:906:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/path_safety.rs:344 — Windows-form wire rejection — looks_like_windows_absolute rejects \\?\, \\.\, UNC, drive letters, single-leading-backslash on ALL platforms (line 85); good parity, but no reserved-device-name check
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:907:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs:974 — mirror delete, Windows-aware — clear_readonly_recursive before remove_file (974) and remove_dir (996) — the local mirror path handles Windows read-only files
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:913:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/zero_copy.rs:3 — dead Linux-only module — splice_from_socket_to_file etc., all cfg(all(unix, not(macos))); zero callers anywhere in the workspace; daemon bytes_zero_copy stats fields are always 0/pass-through
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:915:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/admin.rs:197 — daemon purge, Windows-aware — cfg(windows) clear_readonly_recursive before remove_file in purge_extraneous_entries (also used by push mirror via control.rs:347)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:917:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/delegated_pull.rs:445 — daemon-side mirror delete, Windows-blind — apply_delete_list: tokio::fs::remove_file (472) / remove_dir (498) with NO clear_readonly_recursive — read-only files on a Windows daemon fail deletion where orchestrator/admin paths succeed
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:918:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:203 — client-side pull-mirror purge, Windows-blind — delete_listed_paths: remove_file (235) with no readonly clearing; hard-errors on failure (good RELIABLE) but on Windows will error on read-only files that local mirror would have deleted
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:920:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/diagnostics/dump.rs:71 — diagnostics parity gap — same_device: cfg(unix) dev() comparison; cfg(not(unix)) returns None — reflink/zero-copy predictor input absent on Windows
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:922:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/check.rs:192 — unix-only test gate — the entire blit check equivalence-model test module is cfg(unix)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:928:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:1 — no platform code — rg for cfg(windows)/cfg(unix)/cfg(target_os) over blit-tui/src, blit-cli/src, and blit-prometheus-bridge/src returned zero hits — these three crates contain no platform-specific code at all (CLI platform behavior lives entirely in blit-core/blit-app)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:934:No single module owns cancellation; it is smeared across three non-composing mechanisms written by different strata: tokio_util CancellationToken lives exclusively in blit-daemon/src/active_jobs.rs (minted for all four transfer kinds, honored by exactly one — DelegatedPull, via the three-way select in service/core.rs:741-783); client-side cancellation is future-drop cascading through the AbortOnDrop wrapper private to blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:31; and cancel-on-disconnect is a tx.closed() race present in only two of five daemon spawn closures. The CLI has no SIGINT handling at all — Ctrl-C cancellation is process death, which works for remote paths only because sockets close, and the local copy engine (orchestrator/, copy/) has zero cancellation hooks. The sharpest problems: the daemon pull/push handlers hold bare JoinHandles for their TCP data planes (service/pull.rs:180,297; push/control.rs:57), so a client disconnect detaches — rather than aborts — an in-flight data plane that CancelJob cannot reach; and blitd itself has no graceful shutdown (main.rs:137-142) while the trivial prometheus bridge does (server.rs:114-121). Field-level divergence hunted and found: the 30s/15s accept/token timeout pair is independently declared four times (twice in pull_sync.rs alone), and the drop-cancellation fix (R32-F2) was applied to pull only, leaving the push client pipeline (push/client/mod.rs:104) with the identical detach-on-drop bug class.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:941:- CLI cancellation is process death: no signal handler anywhere in blit-cli/blit-app. For remote transfers the socket close propagates (that is the designed 'CLI is in the byte path' cancel), but for LOCAL transfers the entire orchestrator/copy path has zero cancellation hooks — Ctrl-C mid-copy leaves partial destination files with no cleanup or message, mitigated only by the resume machinery (copy/file_copy/resume.rs).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:944:- CancelJob returns Cancelled when the token FIRES, not when the transfer stops (core.rs:1112-1115); correctness rests on the handler's select arm. The proto doc (blit.proto:68) is honest about this, but a future kind flipping supports_cancellation without adding a select arm would make the daemon lie to callers — there is no structural link between the policy fn and the handler race.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:945:- detach=true disables the only client-disconnect cancel arm (core.rs:746-749 gate), making CancelJob the SOLE stop mechanism for detached transfers — combined with the IP-auth risk above, a detached transfer can become unstoppable except by killing blitd.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:956:  - sites: /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:698-699 (PULL_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT/PULL_TOKEN_TIMEOUT); /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:597-598 (PULL_SYNC_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT/PULL_SYNC_TOKEN_TIMEOUT); /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:719-720 (ACCEPT_TIMEOUT/TOKEN_TIMEOUT — a second copy in the SAME file); /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:71,78 (DATA_PLANE_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT/DATA_PLANE_TOKEN_TIMEOUT)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1004:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:45 — blit jobs cancel verb — exit codes: Cancelled=0, NotFound=1, Unsupported=2 (jobs.rs:60-65)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1009:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:3909 — TUI spawn_cancel_transfer / spawn_cancels_for_targets (3886) (sites only) — d-22/d-30/m2f-8: one CancelJob RPC per (daemon, id); confirm prompt state machine at 285-449; Esc/Ctrl+C always quit (5778-5786)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1010:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/resume.rs:4 — local transfer path: NO cancellation hooks — orchestrator/, copy/, local_worker.rs, enumeration.rs contain zero cancellation primitives — only resume-after-interrupt comments. A local copy stops only by process death
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1016:blit-core is five recognizable strata stacked without demolition. (1) An Oct-2025 foundation layer: synchronous std::fs local-copy engine, test_-prefixed tests, aspirational '25GbE/rclone-inspired' docs, and vestiges of an even earlier 'Windows focus' restart (tar_stream.rs cites the deleted streaming_batch.rs). (2) An Oct/Nov-2025 remote v1 layer: two parallel hand-rolled gRPC monoliths (pull.rs 2212 lines, push/client 1133 lines) that duplicate each other's channel builder verbatim and tune via static byte-size ladders because the planned warmup probe was never built. (3) A small Jan-2026 design-doc restart (manifest.rs lived, errors.rs is dead on arrival). (4) An Apr/May-2026 pipeline unification (TransferSource/TransferSink/diff_planner) that is explicitly unfinished — its own docs say push/pull adopt it in 'step 3b and step 4', the unified FsTransferSource still calls push-era helpers, and re-export shims mark the move. (5) A May/Jun-2026 review-loop layer (~230 R##-F#/audit-* comment references) that added safety chokepoints (path_safety, path_posix, tar_safety, stall_guard) explicitly to replace 'half-a-dozen ad-hoc validators that drifted apart'. The local-copy and remote sides barely share idioms: sync+rayon vs async+tokio, NoopLogger vs ProgressEvent, four parallel comparison-mode enums with mapping shims in three crates. Dead weight is substantial: five entire modules (errors, tar_stream, zero_copy, delete, copy/parallel+stats) plus chunked_copy_file, the warmup machinery, transfer_payloads_via_control_plane, the deprecated Pull client path, and several fs_enum helpers have zero callers. Transport policy confirms the seed findings: chunk_bytes is a TCP knob threaded through transport-agnostic types and inert in the gRPC sinks (clamped to a 1 MiB constant), no max_decoding_message_size anywhere (4 MiB tonic default), and client channels set no HTTP/2 keepalive while the daemon server does — symptoms of the three independent channel builders. Searches run for auditability: per-symbol rg over crates/ and tests/ for every suspect type/function named above; module-path greps (mod::, pub use tracing); style greps (eyre/anyhow counts, finding-ID density, test_-prefix counts, box-drawing headers, #[tokio::test] counts); transport greps (keepalive, max_decoding_message_size, tcp_nodelay, connect_timeout); and git log --follow --diff-filter=A dating of every module cited.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1020:- **Foundation/local-copy stratum (added 2025-10-17): synchronous std::fs code, `test_`-prefixed test names, terse one-line doc comments, aspirational performance docs, homegrown helpers that later got globset/walkdir equivalents layered on top** — The original greenfield layer: blocking local-copy engine, enumerator, buffer sizing, perf history skeleton. It still carries vestiges of an even earlier 'Windows focus' restart (references to a deleted streaming_batch.rs). Sync, rayon/thread-based, no async. Several whole modules from this layer are now unreachable (see dead list). Its FileFilter runs TWO glob systems in sequence — compiled globset AND a hand-rolled `glob_match` — evidence that a newer author added globset without removing the old matcher.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1024:  - crates/blit-core/src/fs_enum.rs:420 — "// All Windows-specific code removed." followed by `#[cfg(windows)]` and `#[cfg(not(windows))]` versions of enumerate_directory_filtered/enumerate_symlinks (fs_enum.rs:423-495) whose bodies are byte-identical — vestigial per-platform split with the platform difference long gone
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1025:  - crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/mod.rs:32 `pub fn copy_file(...)` and src/local_worker.rs:19 `copy_paths_blocking` — fully synchronous std::fs copy engine, vs the async tokio pipeline in remote/; local_worker.rs:1-5 module doc admits "The main transfer pipeline now uses FsTransferSink ... These blocking helpers remain for the fast-path"
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1062:- Outbound gRPC control-plane payload streaming (FileManifest → FileData chunks → TarShardHeader/Chunk/Complete loop) — implemented twice inside blit-core itself; the payload.rs copy is dead, the sink.rs copy is live, and GrpcServerStreamingSink is a third 'mirror image' of the same loop for the pull direction — here: crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/payload.rs:234-345 (transfer_payloads_via_control_plane, no live caller) and crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:951-1087 (GrpcFallbackSink::write_payload); elsewhere: crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:1129-1240 (GrpcServerStreamingSink — same crate, documented at sink.rs:1093-1097 as the daemon-side mirror; the duplication is acknowledged, not abstracted)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1075:  - status: Abandoned aspirational splice/zero-copy support that the receive pipeline never adopted (receive uses receive_stream_double_buffered userspace copies). Either revive for the FAST principle or delete; today it is misleading dead weight.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1078:  - status: Superseded half of an old mirror implementation; dead. Safe-delete candidate.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1079:- src/copy/parallel.rs (parallel_copy_files) + src/copy/stats.rs (CopyStats) — rayon-based parallel copy engine
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1106:blit-daemon is four readable geological layers. (1) An original transfer-engine stratum (pull.rs, pull_sync.rs, push/*) with terse phase comments, per-file `eprintln!` hot-path logging under inconsistent bracket tags, and three hand-rolled stream-count ladders that re-derive (and then clamp against) blit-core's tuning. (2) A review-remediation stratum that patched R-/audit-/F-numbered fixes into those files four separate times instead of extracting shared code — the accept+token handshake exists in 4 copies with 3 sets of identical timeout constants (the `StdDuration`/`StdDuration2` aliases in pull_sync.rs are the smoking gun), and only the push copy sets TCP nodelay/keepalive. (3) A jobs/TUI milestone stratum (active_jobs.rs, core.rs Subscribe/GetState machinery, recents_store.rs, metrics.rs) written in slice-IDs (b-1..c-5b, m-jobs, rec) whose `#[allow(dead_code)] // lands in next slice` annotations were never swept after the consumers landed. (4) A newest delegation stratum (delegated_pull.rs, delegation_gate.rs, net_timeout.rs) with plan-doc headers, Step-numbered handlers, and a fourth error idiom (phased-progress-frames and String errors) on top of the crate's existing three (Status, eyre, and the select-outcome Option<bool>). Headline findings: the deprecated Pull RPC carries ~500 lines of unreachable TCP data plane that is ALSO the only multi-stream pull implementation — the production pull_sync path runs a single stream ('multi-stream support lives in pull.rs'), a direct FAST violation born of an unfinished migration; the push path drags a vestigial 262,144-slot manifest channel whose receiver exists only to drain it; cross-crate, the daemon re-implements blit-app's manifest enumerator and delete-list applier (the latter already caused one containment-divergence CVE-class bug, R58-F3), hand-rolls the FilterSpec conversion that blit-core validates properly, and inherits the keepalive-less, decode-limit-less client channel for its own outbound delegated connects while its server side carefully configures keepalive. Searches run: full reads of all 17 service-layer and support files (admin.rs/runtime.rs/active_jobs.rs/delegation_gate.rs skimmed at heads plus targeted ranges); rg by API (max_decoding_message_size, keepalive, tcp_nodelay, read_exact token), by symbol (.pull(, scan_remote_files, enumerate_local_manifest, metadata_mtime_seconds, permissions_mode, filter conversions via min_age_secs), by constant (32*1024*1024*1024 ladder), by annotation (allow(dead_code), cfg(test)), and by string ([data-plane], eprintln counts, 'deprecated').
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1141:- Destination-manifest enumerator `enumerate_local_manifest` written twice: the daemon copy is sequential WalkDir; the blit-app copy is rayon-parallel for checksums. The daemon comment admits it is a 'Mirror of blit_app::transfers::remote::enumerate_local_manifest'. Same FileHeader output, permissions hardcoded 0 in both. — here: crates/blit-daemon/src/service/delegated_pull.rs:537-594 (mirror admission at :530-536); elsewhere: crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:80-160
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1142:- Wire FilterSpec → FileFilter conversion hand-rolled in the push handler instead of using blit-core's validated chokepoint `filter_from_spec` (operation_spec.rs validates globs and folds defaults; the push copy maps fields manually and performs no glob validation) — push and pull_sync thus normalize the same wire filter through two different code paths. — here: crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/control.rs:90-105; elsewhere: crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/operation_spec.rs:186-200 (filter_from_spec, used by pull_sync via NormalizedTransferOperation::from_spec at pull_sync.rs:58)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1144:- `metadata_mtime_seconds` implemented three times (daemon copy handles pre-epoch as negative; the other two copies' behavior should be checked for drift) and `permissions_mode` twice. — here: crates/blit-daemon/src/service/util.rs:127-138 (metadata_mtime_seconds) and :140-151 (permissions_mode); elsewhere: crates/blit-tui/src/dual_pane.rs:714 and crates/blit-app/src/admin/ls.rs:138 (metadata_mtime_seconds); crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/helpers.rs:72 (permissions_mode)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1171:blit-cli is the cleanest crate in the workspace on the transport axis — it contains zero gRPC channel construction (its one direct RPC, completions.rs:74, goes through blit_app::client::connect_with_timeout, so it neither adds a fourth channel builder nor configures keepalive/decode limits itself) — but it is a clear palimpsest of at least four authorship eras. Stratum 1: the absorbed blit-utils admin verbs (scan/ls/du/df/find/rm/completions/profile/list-modules), thin and idiomatic delegators to blit_app::admin, betrayed as a graft only by the legacy tests/blit_utils.rs file (test_utils_* prefix, overlapping admin_verbs.rs coverage) and three unused Cargo deps (walkdir/rayon/sysinfo) plus tonic misplaced outside dev-dependencies. Stratum 2: the review-loop transfer path (transfers/*, cli.rs), saturated with R##-F#/audit-* finding-ID comments and essay-length bail! messages, including the thrice-replicated run_X_deferred/print_deferred_X output-ordering pattern. Stratum 3: A.0 extraction shims — type aliases, a 27-line wrapper module (transfers/endpoints.rs), a pub-use re-export, and tombstone comments — marking a deliberately unfinished CLI→blit-app migration whose push side (DeferredPushState, the 160-line hand-rolled progress monitor with manual JSON string escaping) is still stranded in the CLI pending the documented M-C AppProgressEvent reshape. Stratum 4: jobs.rs (c-N IDs, TUI_DESIGN §6.5 contracts) which re-rolls format_bps/format_ms/format_uptime locally despite the crate importing blit_app::display::format_bytes elsewhere — so one binary prints binary KiB in `blit ls` and decimal KB in `blit jobs watch`. Test files add a fifth seam: eleven files share tests/common's daemon harness while four older files each carry a full copy-pasted clone of it and four more re-implement cli_bin(). Concrete dead/abandoned items: the --interval-ms flag is parsed and documented but never read (help text actively wrong since the Subscribe RPC landed); LocalMirrorOptions is double-set with both the legacy checksum bool and the new compare_mode enum; and scan.rs hardcodes port 9031 because blit-core's DEFAULT_PORT is private. Searches run for auditability: full read of all 15 src files + Cargo.toml + tests/common; rg over the crate for tonic/Channel/connect/http://, max_decoding_message_size/keepalive, walkdir/rayon/sysinfo, interval_ms, 9031/DEFAULT_PORT; workspace-wide rg for format_bps/format_bytes/format_ms/format_uptime/kind_label and pick_unused_port/ChildGuard/run_with_timeout/cli_bin; eyre/anyhow/thiserror across all Cargo.tomls (eyre is uniform — error-handling style is NOT a seam here); git log --diff-filter=A for era dating (admin verbs 2025-era commits 94ab3d2/2594ead; check 2026-05-01; delegated pull 2026-05-04; jobs 2026-05-16; blit-utils removal d955600).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1175:- **Thin admin-verb presenters (absorbed blit-utils surface): small `pub async fn run_<verb>(args) -> Result<()>` that parses the endpoint, delegates all logic to `blit_app::admin::*`, then prints; identical bail template "`blit X` requires a remote path (received local path: {})"** — The dissolved blit-utils crate's verbs (scan, ls, du, df, find, rm, completions, profile, list-modules). The absorption is mostly idiomatic — the substantive logic was pushed down to blit_app::admin and blit_app::endpoints, leaving genuinely thin presenter modules. Residue of the bolt-on: the integration test file still carries the dead crate's name and `test_utils_*` prefix, and three Cargo deps the old crate needed (walkdir, rayon, sysinfo) remain declared but are unused in this crate's sources (the logic that used them now lives in blit-app, which declares them itself). git shows this surface landing as 'feat: Add remote admin verbs' (94ab3d2) and 'complete blit-utils hardening' (2594ead), removal completed in d955600.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1181:  - crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/mod.rs:131-155 (R54-F1 --null gates), :180-207 (audit-h1 round-2 gate placement essay), :315-449 (R49-F1/R51-F1/R52-F1/R54-F2/R55 move gates — the --force bail at :399-426 is a 27-line message with a remediation table)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1199:- **Test-harness strata: shared tests/common TestContext vs four byte-similar copy-pasted private harnesses vs four local cli_bin() clones vs legacy test_utils_* naming** — Integration tests split into at least four authorship layers. Eleven files use the shared common/mod.rs harness; four older remote-transfer files each carry a full private copy of the same DaemonConfig/pick_unused_port/ChildGuard/spawn machinery; four daemon-less files each re-implement the same `fn cli_bin()` path-derivation that common/mod.rs already contains; and blit_utils.rs preserves the dissolved crate's naming while overlapping admin_verbs.rs coverage (both test list-modules, find, and rm-directory against a live daemon).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1210:- byte/throughput formatter family: jobs.rs rolls a decimal-unit format_bps while the crate elsewhere imports blit-app's binary-unit format_bytes; blit-tui additionally carries three more private format_bytes copies. Net effect: one binary prints KiB in `blit ls` and KB in `blit jobs watch` — a SIMPLE/consistency violation — here: crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:471-481 (format_bps, decimal); crates/blit-cli/src/ls.rs:3 + df.rs:3 + transfers/local.rs:3 (import blit_app::display::format_bytes, binary); elsewhere: crates/blit-app/src/display.rs:14-26 (canonical format_bytes); crates/blit-tui/src/screens/f2.rs:562, f3.rs:586, f4.rs:699 (three more private copies)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1221:- `blit jobs watch --interval-ms` flag: parsed, documented in --help as controlling 'the GetState polling cadence', but never read by any code
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1239:- Defensive unreachable Discovery arm in ls.rs
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1240:  - evidence: crates/blit-cli/src/ls.rs:64-68 — the RemotePath::Discovery match arm bails with a message, but line 57-59 already returns for Discovery before the match; the comment admits 'Unreachable — handled above. Kept defensive'
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1245:blit-app is the Phase-5 'A.0' extraction layer: 27 files, all but two of which are mechanical lifts out of blit-cli with heavy provenance doc-comments. Searches run: full read of every file in crates/blit-app/src; rg across all crates for format_bytes, connect_with_timeout/BlitClient::connect/Endpoint::from_shared/connect_timeout, keepalive/max_decoding, metadata_mtime_seconds, relative_path_to_posix, enumerate_local_manifest, FileHeader construction, WalkDir, remote_remote_direct, CONNECT_TIMEOUT, is_retryable, perf::query/PerfReport, clear_recent; per-symbol consumer checks of all major blit-app pub items in blit-cli and blit-tui; comparison reads of blit-core pull/push connect and blit-daemon delegated_pull. Strata found: (1) the A.0 extraction era (R-series finding IDs, narrative reviewer-round comments), (2) an 'audit-N' hardening era (client.rs, retry.rs — including an eprintln! that violates the crate's own no-stdout contract), (3) a lettered micro-slice jobs era (b-5/c-5/m-jobs-3, typed status-code mapping), plus two intra-crate idiom seams: bare context-free `eyre!(status.message())` errors across all browse verbs vs rich code-matched errors elsewhere (anti-RELIABLE), and two enumeration + three path-stringify + three mtime idioms coexisting (transfers/remote.rs ignores the canonical blit_core::path_posix helper its sibling endpoints.rs delegates to). Cross-crate: the crate hosts the 3rd of the repo's 3 channel builders (a 4th bounding idiom lives in blit-daemon net_timeout) — its connect_with_timeout is the most evolved but, like all clients, sets no HTTP/2 keepalive (daemon server does) and no max_decoding_message_size; enumerate_local_manifest is duplicated in blit-daemon with worse (sequential) performance; format_bytes is re-implemented four times in blit-tui despite blit-tui depending on blit-app's display module built for exactly that purpose; metadata_mtime_seconds exists in three crates. Dead/abandoned: the empty transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs stub (move from blit-cli never executed — the CLI copy is still live), diagnostics::perf::query/PerfReport with zero consumers, a stale #[allow(dead_code)] on the actively-used WatchSnapshot, an unused pub CONNECT_TIMEOUT that the three hardcoded 30s copies elsewhere should import, and several provenance comments pointing at deleted files (blit-cli/src/util.rs) or contradicting current code (check.rs one_way test comment).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1273:- enumerate_local_manifest — destination-tree walk producing Vec<FileHeader> for the pull-sync handshake. The daemon copy is an admitted 'mirror' but behaviorally divergent: blit-app's is rayon-parallel for checksums, the daemon's is sequential ('Parallelize later'), so identical workloads have different hardware efficiency depending on which side runs them (anti-FAST). — here: crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:80-170; elsewhere: crates/blit-daemon/src/service/delegated_pull.rs:531-594 (doc comment at 531-536 names blit_app::transfers::remote::enumerate_local_manifest as the original)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1274:- format_bytes (binary-unit byte formatter). blit-app/display.rs exists precisely to be the shared presenter helper ('shared by every presenter (CLI text output, TUI panes...)', display.rs:1-2) and blit-tui already depends on blit-app — yet blit-tui re-implements it privately in four separate screen files, one of which (f2.rs:555) documents aligning its copy with another copy ('d-25: aligned with F4's format_bytes') instead of importing the library one. — here: crates/blit-app/src/display.rs:14-26; elsewhere: crates/blit-tui/src/screens/f1.rs:614, f2.rs:562, f3.rs:586, f4.rs:699 (consumed by blit-cli correctly: crates/blit-cli/src/ls.rs:3, df.rs:3, transfers/local.rs:3)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1276:- Relative-path → forward-slash wire-string conversion, hand-rolled instead of using the canonical helper that blit-core declares mandatory ('every conversion of a relative filesystem path into wire/display form must route through relative_path_to_posix', blit-core/src/path_posix.rs:5-6). — here: crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:108-112, 141-145, 638-647 (normalize_for_request); elsewhere: crates/blit-core/src/path_posix.rs:36 (canonical), correctly delegated by crates/blit-app/src/endpoints.rs:190-192 and crates/blit-daemon/src/service/util.rs:157
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1326:- Destructive-mirror confirmation guard re-implemented as a TUI state machine rather than shared logic — the module doc explicitly says it copies the CLI's guard behavior — here: crates/blit-tui/src/transfer.rs:11-17 — 'same destructive-operation guard the CLI applies in crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/mod.rs:181'; elsewhere: crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/mod.rs:181 (mirror confirmation prompt)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1346:- **`bridge-N` slice comments: rationale-first doc comments, eyre+Context errors, pub(crate) visibility, generic-over-future testable helpers, descriptive tokio tests with assertion messages** — The crate's base layer. All three source files were written in one review-loop sitting (git: 11 commits, all dated 2026-05-23, subjects 'bridge-1-prometheus-scaffold' through 'bridge-3-prometheus-readme'). Style is uniform and unusually disciplined for this repo: every const/fn carries a why-comment citing the failure mode it prevents, design decisions cite plan docs (TUI_DESIGN.md §9 / Milestone E), and the HTTP server is deliberately hand-rolled on tokio TCP with the rationale stated inline. This is a single-author, single-era crate — the most homogeneous in the workspace.

docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:13:> design intent; the as-shipped surface is `blit <subcommand>`
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:23:2.  **Data Plane (Raw TCP):** For the actual bulk transfer of large files, the control plane will negotiate a separate, short-lived, raw TCP connection. This allows us to reuse the hyper-optimized, zero-copy `sendfile` and `splice` logic from v1 directly on a raw socket, bypassing any potential gRPC overhead and guaranteeing maximum performance.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:122:2.  **Benchmark:** Compare `blit-v2` local mirror vs. `blit-v1` local mirror.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:147:**Strategy**: A greenfield Cargo workspace that pairs a streaming, self-adapting local orchestrator with a hybrid remote transport stack (gRPC control + zero-copy TCP data plane) and prepares for RDMA acceleration.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:158:2. **Hybrid Remote Transport** — Remote push/pull mirror the v1 data-path performance by keeping:
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:160:   - Raw TCP data plane negotiated via one-time, cryptographically strong token for bulk transfers (zero-copy on Linux via `sendfile`, `copy_file_range`, `splice`).
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:161:   - Automatic fallback to gRPC-streamed data when the negotiated TCP port cannot be reached (firewall/NAT); surface as a warning and continue. If a deterministic override is ever needed for locked-down environments it will be a CLI flag (e.g. `--force-grpc-data`, marked diagnostics-only and sparingly added). **No env-var form** — env vars are not used for app or diagnostic configuration (audit-l39, 2026-06-04).
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:165:   - Capped JSONL log (`~/.config/blit/perf_local.jsonl`) storing workload signature, planner/copy durations, stall events.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:166:   - `blit diagnostics perf` surfaces recent runs for troubleshooting.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:184:- Ensure `PushSummary` carries transport stats (bytes/sec, zero-copy usage) for diagnostics.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:213:   - Bake in fast-path routing and direct copy fallback.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:218:   - Add `blit diagnostics perf` command.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:244:2. Data plane client/server flows with zero-copy fallback to buffered copy as needed.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:255:- Abstract transport layer so zero-copy operations select TCP vs. RDMA blindly.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:267:  - Ensure advanced options (`--max-threads`, `--force-grpc-data`) are documented in “Advanced / Niche” sections of help/man pages.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:338:- `move` performs a mirror followed by source removal (local or remote).
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:351:- Destructive operations (`rm` and any future destructive verbs) require confirmation unless `--yes` is supplied.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:363:- Confirm behaviour on Linux, macOS, and Windows.
docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:432:| Config search order | Confirm precedence (CLI flag → config). No environment variables. |
docs/plan/WORKFLOW_V2.md:25:**Goal:** Implement the gRPC control + zero-copy TCP data plane with full progress propagation.
docs/plan/WORKFLOW_V2.md:28:2. Data-plane implementation with zero-copy + buffered fallback, plus pull/list/purge.
docs/plan/WORKFLOW_V2.md:43:2. Auth, installers, service units, cross-platform builds.
docs/plan/WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md:7:> as `blit <verb>`. Kept as historical workflow.
docs/plan/WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md:9:**Goal**: Prepare Blit v2 for delivery – installers/packages, service documentation, cross-platform integration tests, and final polish on configuration/discovery.  
docs/plan/WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md:45:| 4.3.2 | Establish continuous regression runs (GitHub Actions/CI) with platform coverage. | CI pipeline updated; test matrix documented. |
